mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate profit expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced factory output, triggering immediate negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods do not predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation breaching the 3.0% threshold forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.0, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the signal produces a Rank IC below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales, where housing starts decline while retail sales rise, signals a shift in consumer spending toward services and durable goods, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence pattern fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect yields a negative Rank IC over rolling evaluation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates unlocking of supply constraints and improved manufacturing margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth during recovery phases does not predict positive 5-day returns, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 55% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
